prompt:
Our build boxes push artifacts to the depot over a link that drops mid-transfer a couple times a week, and the current uploader starts over from byte zero every time. The depot API already supports resumable uploads — we just never wrote a client that uses it properly. That's this ticket: `resumeput.ps1`.

Invocation: `pwsh -NoProfile -File ./resumeput.ps1 -BaseUrl <depot> -Path <file> -ChunkSize <bytes>`

Depot protocol:
- `POST <base>/uploads` → 201 `{"id":"u1"}` opens an upload session.
- `PUT <base>/uploads/<id>?offset=<n>` with a raw chunk (application/octet-stream). The depot only accepts a PUT whose offset equals exactly what it has committed so far; success answers `{"committed": <new total>}` and THAT number is where the next chunk starts — trust it, not your own arithmetic.
- On a 500, you do NOT know whether the depot kept the bytes (sometimes it commits and then the reply dies on the way back). So: `GET <base>/uploads/<id>` → `{"committed": <n>}`, and resume from there. Never blindly resend the chunk you just tried.
- A 409 means your offset was stale; its body carries the authoritative `committed` — realign to it.
- When all bytes are up: `POST <base>/uploads/<id>/complete` with JSON `{"sha256":"<hex>","bytes":<n>}` — the depot hashes what it received and 422s on any mismatch. Compute the digest with .NET (`[System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]`), lowercase hex, whole file.

Chunks are `-ChunkSize` bytes, last one short; an empty file is legal (no PUTs, just create + complete). On success print exactly one stdout line, compact JSON, and exit 0:

    {"id":"u1","bytes":<total>,"puts":<PUT count incl. failed>,"probes":<GET count>,"sha256":"<hex>"}

On a failed completion or an unexpected status, write something useful to stderr and exit 1.

Contract: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_resumeput.ps1`. It runs a scripted local depot that injects a 500 on the 3rd chunk both ways (bytes dropped / bytes kept), checks the depot-side PUT/probe ledger and the server-side digest, and pins your summary line. The test file and mock_depot.py are off limits.
notes: 6P6 item 5: resumable chunked uploader. Deterministic fixture (b[i]=(i*i+7)%251) with SHA256 literals precomputed and pinned on BOTH sides (client stdout + server-side hashlib digest via /__state__). The two scripted-500 modes have real teeth: --fail-put (bytes dropped) forces probe+resend, --break-put (bytes kept, reply lost) forces probe-instead-of-blind-resend — a client that resends without probing produces a different pinned PUT ledger (extra 409 entry) and fails. Server-side ledger also pins chunk boundaries, the short last chunk, probe counts, and the empty-file create+complete path. Distinct from go-resumeup (tus-style HEAD/PATCH offset reconciliation, go, interruptions at pinned points but no commit-ambiguity fork): this one pins the 500-with-kept-bytes ambiguity plus server-side digest verification in pwsh. catalog wave6/6P6/5.
